instruction: Create a Blueprint with an actor array Targets. On BeginPlay, loop through each actor, get the distance to it, and if closer than 500 print Nearby.

Create a Blueprint with an actor array Enemies. On BeginPlay loop through and find the closest enemy. Store the closest distance in a variable ClosestDist starting at 99999. Print the final closest distance.

Create a Blueprint with vector variables PointA at (0,0,0) and PointB at (500,0,0) and boolean MovingToB defaulting true. On Tick, if MovingToB is true lerp toward PointB, else lerp toward PointA. When close enough to the target, flip MovingToB.

Create a Blueprint that moves an actor back and forth along the X axis between 0 and 1000 units. Use a float variable Progress starting at 0. On Tick add DeltaSeconds to Progress, use a sine wave to calculate X position, and set the actor location.
Create a Blueprint with a float GravityScale variable defaulting to 2.0. On BeginPlay enable physics simulation, then on Tick apply a downward force multiplied by GravityScale every frame.
Create a Blueprint that on pressing PhysicsToggle input, toggles physics simulation on and off using a FlipFlop. When physics turns on print Physics Enabled, when off print Physics Disabled.

Create a Blueprint with a Gate. On pressing Open, open the gate and print Gate Opened. On pressing Close, close the gate and print Gate Closed. On Tick enter the gate, and when the exit fires print Gate Active.
Create a Blueprint with a Gate. On BeginPlay open the gate. On overlap enter the gate. The gate exit prints Passed Through. On pressing Lock input, close the gate and print Gate Locked.

Create a Blueprint for a damage zone. On overlap begin, start a looping timer called DealDamage every 1 second. On overlap end, clear the timer. Create a custom event DealDamage that subtracts 10 from Health and prints the health.

Create a Blueprint for a healing zone. On overlap start a looping timer HealTick every 2 seconds. On overlap end clear it. The HealTick custom event adds 5 to Health, clamps to MaxHealth of 100, and prints Healed to X.
Create a Blueprint for a collectible pickup. On overlap, cast to Character. If cast succeeds, add 1 to a Score variable, print the score, play a sound, then destroy the actor.

Create a Blueprint for a health pickup. On overlap cast to Character. If cast succeeds, add 25 to Health variable (max 100), print Healed, then destroy self. If Health is already 100, print Already Full and don't destroy.
Create a Blueprint that on pressing Spawn input, gets the actor location, adds 200 units forward, and spawns a BP_Enemy at that location. Print Enemy Spawned.

Create a Blueprint that on BeginPlay spawns BP_Helper. Store it in a SpawnedActor variable. On pressing Check input, use IsValid on SpawnedActor. If valid print Helper Alive, if not print Helper Gone.

Create a Blueprint that on BeginPlay spawns a BP_Turret, stores it in a TurretRef variable. On pressing Destroy input, check if TurretRef is valid. If valid, destroy it and print Turret Destroyed. If not valid print No Turret.

Create a Blueprint that on pressing Start input, starts a looping timer Pulse every 0.5 seconds. On pressing Stop, clears the timer. The Pulse custom event prints Pulse and increments a Counter variable.
Create a Blueprint that on BeginPlay calculates: take 100, subtract 30, multiply by 2, add 15, divide by 5, and print the final result.
Create a Blueprint that on BeginPlay creates WBP_Loading widget, adds to viewport, waits 3 seconds, removes the loading widget, creates WBP_MainMenu, adds to viewport, and prints Ready.

Create a Blueprint for a pressure plate. On overlap, if the overlapping actor is valid and is a Character (cast), set a boolean Pressed to true and print Plate Activated. On end overlap set Pressed to false and print Plate Deactivated.

Create a Blueprint for a moving platform. Use float variable Progress starting at 0. On Tick, add DeltaSeconds multiplied by Speed variable (default 1.0) to Progress. Use a sine of Progress to calculate Z offset between 0 and 300, make a vector, and set actor location.
